name: Bringg Delivery Management Platform API
what_it_does: Bringg delivery management platform REST API for last-mile delivery orchestration, fleet dispatch, and carrier management for retail, grocery, logistics, and food delivery businesses. Enables AI agents to manage delivery order creation and fleet or carrier assignment for last-mile delivery dispatch automation, handle route optimization and multi-stop delivery scheduling for delivery efficiency automation, access real-time driver tracking and ETA calculation for customer delivery visibility automation, retrieve customer delivery notification and communication for post-purchase delivery experience automation, manage carrier connectivity and external fleet integration for multi-carrier last-mile orchestration, handle proof of delivery and electronic signature capture for delivery confirmation automation, access driver mobile app workflow and job assignment for field delivery operations automation, retrieve delivery analytics and SLA performance reporting for logistics KPI automation, manage customer returns and reverse logistics for return pickup automation, and integrate Bringg with Salesforce Commerce, Shopify, SAP, Oracle, and WMS platforms for end-to-end order-to-delivery automation.
use_cases: Dispatching deliveries from last-mile orchestration agents
Tracking drivers from real-time visibility automation agents
Notifying customers from delivery experience agents
Optimizing routes from multi-stop efficiency agents
not_for: Long-haul freight and LTL carrier management
best_when: A retailer, grocery chain, third-party logistics provider, or food delivery platform wanting AI agents to automate last-mile delivery dispatch, route optimization, driver management, and customer delivery notifications using Bringg's delivery orchestration platform.
avoid_when: DRIVER BACKGROUND CHECK AND SAFETY COMPLIANCE: Automated driver onboarding and job assignment via Bringg must verify driver background check status and vehicle insurance; automated dispatch of unverified drivers creates customer safety liability and insurance coverage gap. PROOF OF DELIVERY LEGAL VALIDITY FOR HIGH-VALUE SHIPMENTS: Automated electronic signature capture via Bringg for high-value deliveries must meet applicable legal standards for signature validity; automated signature capture without identity verification for pharmacy, age-restricted, or high-value items creates delivery compliance and liability issues. GDPR DRIVER LOCATION TRACKING FOR EU OPERATIONS: Automated continuous driver GPS tracking via Bringg for EU-based operations requires GDPR-compliant driver consent and work council notification in applicable EU jurisdictions; automated driver tracking without employment law compliance creates GDPR and labor law violation in France, Germany, and other EU countries.

auth.notes: Bringg uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. REST API with JSON. Tel Aviv, Israel HQ with US offices. Founded 2013 by Lior Sion and Roy Confino. Private ($100M+ raised, e.ventures, GV, M12 Microsoft Ventures). Global delivery management for retail, grocery, QSR, logistics. 400+ enterprise clients globally. Real-time driver app, carrier connectivity, route optimization. Walmart, The Home Depot, Coca-Cola, Burger King clients. Competes with Onfleet, Routific, and DispatchTrack for last-mile delivery management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise subscription by delivery volume
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual enterprise subscription by order/delivery volume; carrier connectivity and analytics modules; implementation project fees
pricing.notes: Tel Aviv / US. Private ($100M+ raised). Founded 2013. 400+ enterprise clients. Annual subscription by delivery volume. Enterprise implementation project fees.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; order and driver queries throttled; webhooks for delivery events

known_agent_gotchas: CARRIER AVAILABILITY CHECK BEFORE ORDER ASSIGNMENT: Automated carrier assignment via Bringg for external carrier integration must verify carrier capacity and service area coverage before order dispatch; automated order assignment to carrier without availability check creates delivery failure at carrier acceptance stage

GDPR DRIVER TRACKING EMPLOYEE CONSENT FOR EU: Automated continuous GPS tracking of EU-based drivers via Bringg requires GDPR employee consent and in some jurisdictions (France, Germany, Netherlands) works council consultation; automated driver tracking deployment without EU employment law review creates GDPR and local labor law violation

PROOF OF DELIVERY AGE VERIFICATION FOR REGULATED ITEMS: Automated POD workflow for alcohol, tobacco, pharmacy, and other age-restricted deliveries must include driver-facing age verification prompt; automated delivery completion without age verification for regulated items creates compliance violation and alcohol/tobacco license risk

ROUTE OPTIMIZATION RECALCULATION ON REAL-TIME EVENTS: Bringg route optimization is performed at dispatch; real-time traffic, new order additions, and driver delays require route recalculation; automated workflows assuming static route after dispatch miss dynamic recalculation opportunities; implement recalculation trigger on significant deviation events

WEBHOOK EVENT DEDUPLICATION FOR DELIVERY STATUS: Bringg delivery status webhooks may deliver duplicate events on network recovery; automated downstream actions (inventory update, customer notification, billing) must implement idempotency on Bringg event ID to prevent duplicate customer notifications and order status updates

DRIVER APP CONNECTIVITY FOR REMOTE DELIVERY AREAS: Bringg driver app requires cellular connectivity for real-time tracking and status updates; automated delivery operations in areas with poor cellular coverage create tracking gaps; implement offline mode handling and status sync on connectivity restore for rural delivery operations
agent_readiness.security_notes: Delivery management. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Driver location, delivery, and customer PII data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Last-mile delivery. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Driver, delivery, and customer location data.
